image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: This rare gemstone found by a 12-year-old in Jewish village was cursed by Jesus
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Sri Lankan family faces deportation despite mother's UK visa extension: Children must leave
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Debt, inflation & IMF dependence: Pakistan is still spending $900,000 every month on US lobbying
item.description: Pakistan is spending heavily on lobbying in the United States. This occurs as the nation faces economic difficulties and water scarcity. The country also deals with rising fuel costs. FARA filings reveal extensive efforts to influence Washington. This includes security, trade, and diplomatic outreach. The lobbying intensified during military tensions with India. Pakistan's army chief made claims about mediation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 6 alligator-infested rivers in the US: From the St. Johns River in Florida to the Brazos River in Texas
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What looked like simple red stains turned out to be Britain’s oldest cave art from 17,100 years ago
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Chinese philosopher Confucius: “Before you embark on a journey of revenge, dig two graves.”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: An alternative to the Strait of Hormuz? Why Iran is eyeing Yemen's Bab al-Mandab
item.description: Escalating Middle East tensions are now focusing on the Bab al-Mandab Strait, a crucial global shipping lane. Iran, via its Yemeni allies, may use this chokepoint to pressure adversaries, potentially disrupting vital oil and goods transit. This raises fears of wider conflict impacting international trade routes and energy markets.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Operation Checkmate: 30 Indians held for illegally working as truck drivers in US, deportation likely
item.description: US Border Patrol's Operation Checkmate has led to the arrest of approximately 30 Indian nationals working illegally as commercial truck drivers in Arizona. These individuals, along with others from different countries, are facing deportation. The operation aimed to enhance public safety by enforcing immigration laws and identifying undocumented drivers operating heavy vehicles.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bangladesh: Thousands gather to pay final respects to Awami League veteran, despite ban
item.description: Thousands gathered in Dhaka to honor Awami League veteran Tofail Ahmed, sparking speculation about the party's potential resurgence amidst a ban. This event, coupled with claims of minimal reforms by the interim government and its alignment with certain parties, suggests a possible return for the Awami League. Meanwhile, a minority minister resigned citing health issues.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: Why is HasanAbi barred from entering the UK? Twitch star claims visa was revoked ahead of major appearances
item.description: Twitch streamer HasanAbi has claimed that the United Kingdom revoked his visa, preventing him from attending several planned events and appearances in the country. Speaking during a livestream, he criticized the decision and linked it to broader political issues. The controversy quickly gained attention online, with supporters and critics debating his claims while questions remain about the circumstances behind the reported visa revocation.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Why was Jack Doherty arrested? Viral handcuff video sparks heated debate across social media
item.description: A viral video showing streamer Jack Doherty in handcuffs and being escorted to a police car has sparked widespread debate online. While some viewers were shocked, many social media users questioned the footage's authenticity, calling it a staged stunt. The controversy comes weeks after Doherty claimed he had been placed under house arrest, adding more uncertainty to the situation and fueling speculation across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Hasan Piker says he was denied entry to the UK ahead of major speaking events
item.description: Popular online commentator Hasan Piker and media personality Cenk Uygur were barred from entering the United Kingdom. Their travel permissions were revoked just before scheduled speaking events. This unexpected development led to the cancellation of their appearances at SXSW London and the University of Oxford. The British Home Office stated their presence was not conducive to the public good.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: US politician trolled for his 'HinJew' family photo: 'Your kids are 0% Jewish'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who was Dave Fiji? Indian-origin pilot killed in helicopter crash hours after marrying
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: IShowSpeed’s new World Cup anthem ‘Champions’ has fans calling it FIFA 2026’s unofficial song
item.description: IShowSpeed has launched a new football song titled Champions. The track is for the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. It has quickly gained massive online attention. Fans are strongly supporting Champions to become the official World Cup anthem. The music video has already garnered significant views. This song celebrates the global nature of the tournament.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 87-year-old Florida man dies after Tesla on Autopilot mode crashes into pond
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump says Iran talks continue 'at rapid pace' as Tehran suspends negotiations after Lebanon strikes
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that negotiations with Iran are progressing rapidly despite recent exchanges of fire testing a fragile ceasefire. He also announced a halt to attacks between Hezbollah and Israel following calls with Prime Minister Netanyahu and Hezbollah representatives. Iran, however, indicated it has ceased communication with Washington through mediators due to the escalation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Number 1 mistake people make in visa and Green Card applications, according to immigration experts
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Sigmund Freud: “The virtuous man contents himself with dreaming that which the wicked man does in actual life.”
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Did you know that the Sun looks yellow from Earth but is actually white in space
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
